page: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a local 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. The single-core CPU performance is strong, scoring 2896.39 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core limitation, multi-core performance is weak. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block speeds at 130.9 GB/sec and large block speeds at 22.42 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, registering 27.17 GB/sec and 11.19 GB/sec respectively.

This instance offers a budget-friendly entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained multi-core processing or high memory bandwidth. The shared CPU allocation and lower Passmark database score of 926.43 make it less suitable for high-throughput database operations or intensive web serving. It is best suited for lightweight memory-optimized tasks, basic single-threaded applications, small-scale LLM text generation using small models, or development environments requiring more RAM than standard budget tiers.
description: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with a single shared AMD EPYC vCPU at 2.0 GHz and 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. It includes a 50 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2896.39 in stress-ng, though multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are limited, with large-block read bandwidth at 22.42 GB/sec. This shared-CPU instance represents a cost-efficient option for memory-leaning workloads that do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, development environments, and small-scale text generation tasks.

tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC instance pairing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio with local NVMe storage for budget-conscious, single-threaded workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory providing a high 1:8 resource ratio
